info.title: Totalis RFQ API
info.description: Public REST surface for the Totalis parlay RFQ platform — a decentralized request-for-quote marketplace for parlay bets across Kalshi and Polymarket, with on-chain Solana vault settlement. **Wire format**: snake_case JSON. All successful responses are wrapped in `{ "data": ... }`; list endpoints add `meta` with cursor-based pagination. Errors use a `{ "error": { code, message, details? } }` envelope. **Authentication**: programmatic clients send `X-API-Key`; the web dashboard uses Privy JWTs (`Authorization: Bearer ...`). Any authenticated user can both place parlays and quote as a market maker — there is no separate MM role. Admin endpoints are out of scope for this public reference. **Rate limiting**: 100 req/min anonymous, 300 req/min authenticated. Responses include `X-RateLimit-*` headers. Request body limit: 256KB. **Pagination**: list endpoints use opaque cursor pagination. Pass `meta.cursor` from the previous response as `?cursor=...` to fetch the next page; do not parse or construct cursors client-side.

tags.name: Markets
tags.description: Cached Kalshi and Polymarket market data.

tags.name: User
tags.description: User profile, wallet, and devnet helpers.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Manage programmatic access keys for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by category. Pass 'all' to disable the default category filter.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Filter by venue (kalshi or polymarket). Omit to include all venues.

parameters.name: subcategory
parameters.description: Filter events by subcategory. Valid values depend on the selected `category` (e.g. Crypto → btc/eth/sol/other; Finance → stocks/indices/etfs/earnings). Requires `category` to also be set.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.description: Filter events by the cadence of the underlying series: `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`, or `hourly`. Only meaningful for categories that drive timeframe pills (Crypto, Finance, Economics, Entertainment). Monthly markets are also subject to a 7-day visibility window — see G1 spec §1.1. `hourly` is admitted as a filter post-0048 (after the 19 hourly series were re-enabled). Per-category validity is enforced server-side by the join against `series_whitelist.frequency`: requesting `?frequency=hourly&category=entertainment` returns an empty grid (no hourly entertainment series exist today) rather than 400. `other` is intentionally NOT a valid filter input — it's a residual bucket on `Market.frequency` for irregular-cadence series, not a chip target. Passing `?frequency=other` returns 400.

parameters.name: date_filter
parameters.description: Time bucket filter. If omitted, a default 7-day horizon is applied. Pass 'all' to retrieve all active markets regardless of close date.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Events per page. Default 21 for /markets. The product limit × markets_per_event must not exceed 2000.

parameters.name: markets_per_event
parameters.description: Max markets returned per event group. The full count is always in total_markets. Default 10, max 50. The product limit × markets_per_event must not exceed 2000.

X-Response-Truncated.description: Present when the response was truncated to fit the 2MB size budget. Fewer events than requested are returned; use cursor to fetch more.

available_subcategories.description: Subcategory slugs present in the (category, date_filter, search, frequency) result set, ignoring `subcategory` itself. Omitted when the selected category has no subcategories defined.

available_frequencies.description: Frequency values present in the (category, date_filter, search, subcategory) result set, ignoring `frequency` itself. One of daily/weekly/monthly/hourly in declaration order; never includes `other` (residual bucket, not a filter target). Omitted when the selected category has no timeframe pills (i.e. anything outside Crypto/Finance/Economics/Entertainment). Categories without active hourly series (Economics, Entertainment today) simply omit `hourly` from the array — no special-casing client-side.

get.summary: Get single market by ticker or condition ID
get.description: Returns one Kalshi market by ticker or one Polymarket market by condition ID. When `venue` is omitted, the API infers Polymarket for condition IDs that look like `0x...` and otherwise checks Kalshi.

name.description: Optional display name for the key

preset.description: Scope-bundle preset. The canonical list is SCOPE_PRESETS in api-key-scopes.ts (not re-enumerated here, to avoid drift as presets are added). Overridden by `scopes` if both are sent; defaults to `trading` if neither.

scopes.description: Explicit scope list. Clamped to the creator's own scopes.

confirm_withdraw.description: Required (true) when the requested scopes include `funds:withdraw`.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Referral code to auto-redeem on first login

parameters.name: invite
parameters.description: 22-char invite-link token to auto-redeem on first login

parameters.name: referral
parameters.description: 8-char referral code for attribution and auto-approve on first login

get.description:
Realized P&L per day over the requested window — the `/v1` counterpart of `/app/pnl-chart`,
reachable by API-key principals. Returns the caller's own timeseries. Stats totals (wins/losses/realized P&L) are on
`GET /v1/portfolio`.

Returns the user's full portfolio in a single batched query: vault balance,
trading stats, RFQ status counts, and active-bet summary. Balance is null
when the user has no vault (never traded).
Same as `GET /rfqs`, on the programmatic `/v1` contract surface.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by RFQ status. Repeatable values are joined with commas (e.g. ?status=open,quoted,accepted).

get.summary: Get RFQ by ID (programmatic /v1 surface; quotes always populated)
get.description: Same as `GET /rfqs/{id}`, on the programmatic `/v1` contract surface.

get.summary: Get user vault state and active positions
get.description:
Returns the user's vault balance and active positions. Returns data: null
(200 OK) if the user has no vault yet (vault is created on first trade).

200.description: OK — vault state or null if no vault exists

get.summary: Latest Solana blockhash for client-side transaction construction

post.summary: Confirm TEE wallet delegation AND drain any pending welcome airdrop
post.description:
Two responsibilities, kept in one round-trip so the frontend can
render a single combined success toast:

1. **Delegation status.** Confirms whether the embedded wallet
   is currently delegated to our key quorum. The actual
   delegation is a USER-SIDE action (frontend `addSigners()`);
   this endpoint only reads the current state — it does NOT
   perform the delegation itself. 2. **Pending welcome-airdrop drain.** Under the deferred-airdrop
   flow (TT-airdrop-bug 2026-05-13), the welcome airdrop amount
   is staged on `users.welcome_airdrop_pending_amount` at
   approval time. This endpoint atomically claims that amount,
   mints the USDC from treasury to the canonical wallet, and
   clears the column. Idempotent: a second call after a
   successful drain returns `airdrop: null` (nothing left to
   drain) without re-minting. On mint failure, the pending
   amount is restored so the user can retry on their next call.

airdrop.description:
Outcome of the pending-airdrop drain. `null` when nothing was staged for this
user (most common — pre-approval, or
already drained on a prior call). See
`AirdropDrainOutcome` for the non-null
shape.

post.summary: Submit a client-signed serialized Solana transaction
post.description: Rate-limited: 10 requests/min. Used for delegation and other user-signed transactions.

post.summary: Withdraw USDC from the embedded wallet to an external address
post.description:
Sends USDC out of the caller's embedded wallet ATA to an arbitrary
on-curve Solana address. Uses Privy's session-signer delegation
(set up via /v1/wallet/enable-trading) and Privy's native gas
sponsorship — the user is not prompted to sign and never needs SOL. Chained vault-tap: when the requested `amount_usdc` exceeds the
caller's ATA-available balance, the handler first issues
`withdraw_from_vault` (vault PDA → owner ATA) for the shortfall,
waits for that TX to confirm, then issues the external SPL transfer. The `vault_signature` / `vault_amount` fields surface the intermediate
TX when this happens. If the vault tap succeeds but the external
transfer fails to construct or broadcast, the response is **502**
with `error.code: PARTIAL_VAULT_ONLY` and the vault TX surfaced under
`error.details.vault_signature` / `error.details.vault_amount`. The
caller can retry with the same amount; the pre-flight will see the
ATA is now sufficient and skip the vault tap. 502 (rather than 200)
is deliberate: the idempotency middleware caches only 2xx, so
same-key retries against a 502 re-enter the handler instead of
replaying a cached partial response. Idempotency-Key is honored: a duplicate request with the same key
replays the cached 2xx response without re-issuing on-chain TXs.

recipient.description:
Destination Solana wallet address (base58). Must be
on-curve — PDAs, program IDs, and token-account
addresses are rejected because funds sent to a
derived ATA-of-an-ATA are unrecoverable.

amount_usdc.description:
USDC amount to send. Minimum is one USDC base unit
(1e-6). Must be ≤ the caller's total spendable pool
(ATA + vault free_balance − in-flight RFQ holds);
requests exceeding this are rejected with 400.

200.description:
Withdrawal submitted. `signature` is the external SPL transfer
signature. `vault_signature` and `vault_amount` are present
only when the request required a vault → ATA hop.

explorer_url.description: Solscan URL for the external SPL signature.

vault_signature.description:
On-chain `withdraw_from_vault` signature. Present only when the request required a vault
→ ATA hop to cover a shortfall.

vault_amount.description:
USDC amount moved from vault to ATA before the
external transfer. Present only when
`vault_signature` is present.

recipient_ata_created.description:
Present (true) when the recipient had no USDC token
account and this transaction created one (the
create-and-chargeback path, gated by
WITHDRAW_ATA_AUTOCREATE). The fee-payer fronts the
~0.002 SOL account rent and bills it back in USDC, so
the recipient receives `delivered_usdc` and the treasury
keeps `chargeback_usdc`. The create is non-idempotent, so
a confirmed response means this tx created the account
(a race where it already existed reverts and retries
via the standard path, with no chargeback).

chargeback_usdc.description:
USDC withheld from the requested amount to reimburse
the one-time recipient-ATA rent. Present only with
`recipient_ata_created`.

delivered_usdc.description:
USDC actually delivered to the recipient
(`amount_usdc − chargeback_usdc`). Present only with
`recipient_ata_created`.

409.description:
The caller's embedded wallet is no longer delegated for
server-side signing. Re-enabling trading from the app restores
the session signer.

502.description:
Ambiguous partial state — returned as 5xx (not 200) so
Idempotency-Key callers retry against the handler instead of
replaying a cached partial response. Two codes:

`PARTIAL_VAULT_ONLY` — the vault tap succeeded but the external SPL
transfer failed to construct/broadcast. Funds moved from vault to
ATA; retry the same request to complete the external leg (the
pre-flight sees the updated ATA and skips the vault tap). Returned
only when the vault tap fired this request; a same-key retry against
an already-topped-up ATA skips the tap (`shortfall = 0`) and a
subsequent SPL failure surfaces as a normal 500. `WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED` — only on the recipient-ATA
create-and-chargeback path (WITHDRAW_ATA_AUTOCREATE), where broadcast
and confirmation are separate steps: the transaction was broadcast
(`details.signature` present) but confirmation timed out, so it may
have landed. Do NOT blind-retry — verify the recipient balance first,
since a retry would re-send via the now-existing ATA and double-pay.

signature.description:
The broadcast transaction signature. Present on
WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED — the tx may have
landed; check it before retrying.

chargeback_usdc.description:
On WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED, the USDC that
would have been withheld for the recipient-ATA rent.

delivered_usdc.description:
On WITHDRAW_SUBMITTED_UNCONFIRMED, the USDC that
would have been delivered to the recipient.

CursorParam.name: cursor
CursorParam.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's meta.cursor. Do not construct manually.

DeviceFingerprint.name: X-Device-Fingerprint
DeviceFingerprint.description: Client-computed device fingerprint (e.g. a FingerprintJS visitorId). Optional anti-sybil signal captured for account-linkage clustering and investigation. Surveillance-only — it is stored and linked, never used to gate, limit, or block a request.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Client-generated UUID. Cached responses returned within 24h TTL.

AirdropDrainOutcome.description:
Outcome of the pending-airdrop drain performed by
`POST /v1/wallet/enable-trading`. Narrower than
`AirdropOutcome` — there is no `pending` field because the drain
is the terminal step (either the mint landed or it didn't; the
amount is never left in a staged state by this endpoint). `{ success: true, amount }` — the staged amount minted
successfully on-chain. `{ success: false, amount }` — the mint
attempt failed and the pending amount has been restored; the
user can retry on their next call.

AirdropOutcome.description:
Invite-link airdrop outcome for the auto-redeem / invite-link path. `kind` identifies which grant path produced the outcome so clients
can route welcome and parlay-gated grants to different UI flows. Under the deferred-airdrop flow (TT-airdrop-bug 2026-05-13),
`success: true` with `pending: true` means the amount was staged
for the selected grant path and will mint later when that path's
condition is satisfied. `success: false` (always paired with
`pending: false`) means staging failed; the approval itself
still landed.

pending.description:
True when the amount is staged but not yet minted —
waiting on the relevant grant condition to drain it. False
on the failure branch (`success: false`); the staging write
didn't land so there's nothing to mint.

kind.description:
`welcome` grants mint after wallet delegation via
`POST /v1/wallet/enable-trading`; `parlay` grants mint
after the user's cumulative executed parlay bet_amount
reaches the snapshotted threshold.

ApiKeyFull.description: Returned on creation only (full key visible once)

Cashout.description:
Early-cashout (buyback) summary attached to a bought_back RFQ — the
buyback analogue of Settlement. realized_pnl is the user's net P&L
(stake nets out; the directional `amount` is the realized delta, less
the profit fee on the profiting side and the create-time taker fee).

realized_pnl.description: User's realized P&L from the early cashout (USDC, signed).

mm_pays_user.description: True when the MM paid the user (user was up on the position).

cashed_out_at.description: Time the position was bought back / cashed out (the buyback fee insert = finalize moment). The buyback analogue of settled_at; clients sort the History cashed-out row by this. Absent on legacy rows without a fee.

ready.description: True if a wallet is linked and delegation can proceed

delegated.description: True if TEE wallet delegation is active (server can sign)

address.description: Solana wallet address (base58). Empty string if no wallet linked.

wallet_id.description: Privy embedded wallet ID (used for delegation API calls)

leg_index.description: Zero-based ordering index for the leg within the RFQ

yes_sub_title.description: Snapshot of `Market.yes_sub_title` at RFQ creation (e.g. team name on a sports market). Lets clients render the side-specific subtitle after the underlying market has rolled out of the live `/markets` cache. Null on binary markets that carry no per-side subtitle and on legs created before this field was persisted.

no_sub_title.description: Snapshot of `Market.no_sub_title` at RFQ creation — opposing-side counterpart to `yes_sub_title`. Same null semantics.

venue_url.description: Canonical URL of the leg's market on its source venue (Kalshi or Polymarket). Hydrated from marketd Redis hashes, falling back to the parlay-leg snapshot when the market hash has been purged.

image_url.description: Image URL for the leg's market. Hydrated from the marketd Redis hash and whitelist metadata, falling back to the parlay-leg snapshot when the market hash has been purged.

expected_expiration_time.description: Expected market resolution time for the leg. Hydrated from marketd Redis hashes, falling back to the parlay-leg snapshot when the market hash has been purged.

current_yes_price.description: Yes ask price at RFQ creation time, decimal probability (0-1)

current_no_price.description: No ask price at RFQ creation time, decimal probability (0-1)

bet_group.description: Polymarket-only today: the section label Gamma calls `groupItemTitle` with the parent event-title prefix stripped (e.g. `"Match O/U 21.5"`, `"↑ $200,000"`, `"Oklahoma City Thunder"`). Frontends prefer it as the row's primary label over `yes_sub_title` when set. Null for every Kalshi row (Kalshi's per-market `title` is already informative) and null for Polymarket markets that ARE the event (single-market events / moneyline of a multi-market event — render from `title` instead). Stripping uses an exact-prefix match with a word/punct boundary, falling back to the raw `groupItemTitle` if the prefix doesn't match — never partially stripped. See TT-683.

frequency.description: Cadence of the underlying series: daily | weekly | monthly | hourly | other. NULL only for categories without timeframe pills. All five values can appear on responses; the `?frequency=` filter accepts daily / weekly / monthly / hourly (post-0048 admit) but rejects `other` — see the `frequency` query param description below for the filter-vs-response asymmetry.

price_to_beat.description:
Reference price ("strike") for Polymarket "Up or Down" markets —
the spot at window-start that the final close must exceed for
"Up" to win. Sources, in order:
  1. Gamma `event.eventMetadata.priceToBeat` — populated for
     crypto up/down markets. 2. For stock daily up/down markets, Gamma leaves the field
     null; marketd enriches it from Pyth Hermes (prior
     NYSE trading day's regular-session close — the same source
     Polymarket cites for resolution). See TT-654 /
     `services/marketd/internal/fetchers/pyth/hermes.go`. NULL for Kalshi markets, for non-up/down Polymarket markets,
for up/down markets whose window has not yet opened (common
pre-start for 5m / 15m series), and for unsupported tickers
or transient Pyth failures.

venue_url.description: Canonical market URL regardless of venue (Kalshi or Polymarket)

exclusion_keys.description:
Parlay-correlation keys. Two markets cannot co-exist in the same
parlay **iff their `exclusion_keys` arrays intersect** (share at
least one string). Frontends apply pure set-intersection — they hold
no correlation rule tables of their own; the keys encode every rule
(cross-series exclusion groups, threshold ladders, sports same-game
family/kind/participant correlation, Polymarket physical sub-events). Keys are opaque, namespaced by reason+scope (e.g. `kp:<gameKey>:…`,
`grp:<idx>:<eventKey>`, `fam:…`), and derived solely from this
market, so a selected leg can snapshot its keys and compare later. Does NOT encode the plain one-leg-per-event rule (same
`event_ticker`). The submit-time authority is the server's parlay
validation; these keys mirror it. Empty when the market participates
in no correlation rule.

event_title.description: Venue-side event header (descriptive long form) — Kalshi `event.title` from /events (e.g. "Netflix MMA Special: Rousey vs Carano"); Polymarket `event.title` from Gamma, trimmed. Distinct from `series_title` (the underlying series name) and from each market's per-row `title`. Use for surfaces with room (dialog header). Null for rows synced before this field was persisted or where the venue fetch transiently failed.

event_sub_title.description: Compact event header — Kalshi `event.sub_title` from /events (e.g. "Rousey vs Carano", "SF vs ATH (May 16)", "In Jul 2026"). Always null on Polymarket: Gamma's `event.title` is already compact and has no sub-title analogue. Use for surfaces where space is tight (card row). Null for Kalshi rows synced before this field was persisted; the frontend should fall back to `event_title` in that case.

display_shape.description: Card-rendering discriminant derived per event group in the cache pipeline. The frontend's CARD_VARIANTS map keys on this value to select the component for each event card. Phase 2.2 wires the dispatcher; Phases 3–5 swap in per-shape components.

limit.description: Maximum number of items requested for this page.

offset.description: Number of items skipped before this page.

total.description: Total number of items available across all pages, when returned by the endpoint.

PortfolioBalance.description:
Caller's vault balance (DB-cached, no RPC). Null only when the
caller has no vault row at all. For dual-role accounts the
bettor-side vault is preferred and the maker-side row is used
as a fallback (same rules as `GET /v1/vault`).

balance.description: Null when user has no vault (never traded)

PortfolioSummary.description: Derived portfolio-level aggregates from active positions.

active_bet_total.description: Sum of bet_amount across active RFQs

portfolio_value.description: Gross vault balance (deposited USDC). Does not include unrealized P&L.

PositionStatus.description: Position lifecycle status. On-chain states: Active, SettledWin, SettledLoss, Cancelled, Expired. DB adds intermediate states for async processing: pending, processing, settling, cancelling, error. External-signer (headless) path: commit → awaiting_signature → (client returns the signed tx) → active. Two-step expiry path: active → expired_pending_mm_release → expired (with reconciling_mm_release as short-lived sentinel). Early-cashout (buyback) path: active → bought_back_pending_db → bought_back (with reconciling_bought_back_db as the reconciler sentinel).

quotes.description: null = not requested (fetched without include=quotes). [] = requested but no quotes yet.

position_pda.description: On-chain position address. Set once status >= confirmed.

position_id.description: On-chain position id — the 16-byte vault_positions.position_id, hex-encoded (32 chars). Distinct from position_pda (a base58 address): this is the id the early-cashout endpoints key on (/internal/positions/cashout-eligibility, POST /v1/cashout-requests). Set once status >= confirmed.

position_error.description: Last error recorded on the vault position. Populated when position_status === 'error'; null otherwise.

is_failed.description: True when the vault position errored (on-chain execution failed). Derived from position_status === 'error'. Omitted (not false) for non-failed parlays.

cashout.description: Set on bought_back (early-cashout) RFQs only — realized buyback P&L.

create_position_tx.description: create_position transaction signature. Set once the on-chain vault position is created (status >= confirmed).

settle_tx.description: settle_position transaction signature — the on-chain proof a settled RFQ paid out. Set on settled RFQs (the settle analogue of buyback_tx).

cancel_tx.description: cancel_position transaction signature. Set on cancelled RFQs.

buyback_tx.description: Buyback (early-cashout) transaction signature, surfaced at the RFQ root by the serializer. Set on bought_back RFQs — the on-chain proof of the cashout, the buyback analogue of settle_tx.

executed.description: Positions on-chain but not yet resolved. Distinct from total_settled — settled means market resolution + payout distributed.

total_failed.description: Parlays where the vault position errored on-chain. Derived from vault_positions.status = 'error'.

payout.description:
Full pot transferred to the winner — equals user_stake + mm_risk
(i.e. bet_amount * payout_odds). NOT just the MM-side movement. For MM PnL on a loss, use -(payout - user_stake) = -mm_risk.

user_stake.description:
Bettor's stake at settle time. Surfaced so the MM dashboard can
derive PnL without a second fetch — MM win = +user_stake - fee,
MM loss = -(payout - user_stake) = -mm_risk.

mm_risk.description:
Maker's max risk at settle time. Equal to payout - user_stake; the
MM dashboard renders loss-side PnL directly off this field.

fee_amount.description: Protocol fee deducted from the winner's payout.

SubcategorySlug.description: Subcategory wire format (lowercase snake_case). See `src/market-taxonomy.ts:SUBCATEGORIES_BY_CATEGORY` for the canonical taxonomy keyed by category. Valid values depend on the selected `category` and are enforced server-side.

id.description: Privy DID (did:privy:...) - internal identifier, not exposed on public endpoints

username.description: Display name. Auto-assigned a friendly random handle (e.g. GoldenFalcon) on first login; user can update to a 3-20 char alphanumeric/underscore name via PUT /v1/username.

approved.description: Set to true when user redeems a valid invite code or is admin-approved

waitlisted.description: Always present. true when approved=false and user is on the waitlist; false otherwise.

auto_redeemed.description: Only present when the ?ref param successfully redeemed an invite code in this exact request.

airdrop.description:
Welcome airdrop outcome from the auto-redeem path. Present
alongside `auto_redeemed: true`. `null` is possible when the
invite link/code has no airdrop configured, or when the user
was already credited on a prior approval. See
`AirdropOutcome` for the non-null shape.

updated_at.description: Null when the user has never settled a trade (no rfq.user_stats row yet).

UserVaultResponse.description:
Vault state with active position summaries (GET /v1/vault response). Returns the caller's bettor-side vault (owner_type='user') when one
exists; falls back to their maker-side vault (owner_type='mm') for
pure-MM accounts so the portfolio surface reflects every USDC the
caller controls. `positions` is the bettor-side active list when
returning the user vault, or the maker-side active list (joined via
`q.market_maker_id`) when returning the MM vault.

VaultPositionSummary.description:
Lightweight position summary for the vault response. Caller-relative reading: `user_stake` and `mm_risk` are
side-of-trade labels, not caller-relative ones. When
`UserVaultResponse` returns the bettor-side vault, `user_stake`
is the caller's own stake. When it returns the maker-side
vault (pure-MM accounts), `user_stake` is the counterparty
bettor's stake and `mm_risk` is the caller's own collateral. Don't display `user_stake` as "my stake" without branching on
which vault was returned — for aggregate "USDC committed",
prefer `wallet.locked_amount`, which is role-agnostic.

user_stake.description:
Bettor side of the trade. The caller's stake on the
bettor-side vault response; the counterparty's stake on
the maker-side vault response.

mm_risk.description:
Maker side of the trade. The counterparty's collateral on
the bettor-side vault response; the caller's own
collateral on the maker-side vault response.

address.description: Solana wallet address. Null if no wallet linked.

vault_balance.description:
Gross USDC balance currently held in the caller's Totalis vault. Resolved by the returned `address`; returns 0 when no vault row
exists for that wallet. Includes both free vault cash and locked
collateral. Add to `usdc_balance` for the caller's total on-chain
USDC across wallet ATA and vault; subtract `locked_amount` from
that total for an available-cash view.

locked_amount.description:
Every USDC the caller has committed across roles, summed:
in-flight RFQs (bet_amount of open/quoted/accepted/confirmed
tickets where the caller is the bettor), the caller's
bettor-side active vault position stakes
(`SUM(vault_positions.user_stake)` for active/settling/
cancelling), and the caller's maker-side vault
`locked_collateral`. The FE relies on this single field
rather than re-summing on the client. Reconciliation note: this is the union across every role the
caller owns. `GET /v1/vault.locked_collateral` only reports
one vault (bettor preferred, maker fallback), so for a
dual-role account `locked_amount` can exceed
`/v1/vault.locked_collateral` — do not derive "in positions"
as a subtraction of the two. Balance-math note: `vault_balance` is gross, so vault locked
collateral is intentionally represented in both `vault_balance`
and `locked_amount`. Available cash is
`usdc_balance + vault_balance - locked_amount`; do not add
`/v1/vault.locked_collateral` to `locked_amount` again.

has_traded.description:
True once any of the caller's parlays has reached `executed` or
`settled` (the on-chain vault position landed). Informational
account state; withdrawals are allowed regardless of this value.

PrivyJWT.description: Privy JWT issued to the web dashboard. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`. The Privy session signer underpins all wallet-signed actions.

ApiKey.name: X-API-Key
ApiKey.description: Programmatic API key. Sent as `X-API-Key: <key>`. Generate one from the Totalis dashboard. The same header is accepted on the WebSocket auth message.
